segments.title: Santa leadership and objective-led teams
segments.summary: A Christmas leadership lecture uses Santa as a worked example for clear guidance, objective-led command, skills-first selection, and team coordination.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's leadership framework rather than the later comic 12 Days section. Dr. Clarke starts with clear internal and external guidance, links leadership training to giving subordinates objectives and mission purpose instead of narrow orders, then develops a skills-over-image and team-building argument through Santa, Rudolph, elves, and Krampus.

evidence.note: The opening leadership section connects clear guidance to naval and military examples, including giving subordinates objectives and mission purpose rather than only orders.
The skills-over-image section argues that leaders can fix presentation more easily than commitment, ability, and willingness to learn.
The team-building section treats Santa's wider organization as a lesson in selecting the right skills, coordinating people, and setting respectful boundaries.
